title: Wide Rustic X Kitchen Island
summary: A rolling kitchen island in the Rustic X family — X-braced ends, slatted bottom shelf, two shallow drawers on waxed wood cleats, and a project-panel top on casters.
description:
The Rustic X family's workhorse: a 52"-wide rolling island with X-braced end frames, a slatted lower shelf for stockpots, a middle shelf bay, and two shallow drawers. It's the most involved X build — drawers and a fitted top on top of the usual frame joinery — and the one that earns its place in the kitchen every day.

The drawers ride on the oldest drawer technology there is: wood cleats waxed with a candle. The source is refreshingly practical about why — the drawer bay is under 12" deep and short metal slides in that size are scarce — so 2x2 cleats carry 1x2-cleated drawer boxes, rubbed with candle wax until they glide. Install the cleats with just two screws each at first, because the plan TELLS you you'll be adjusting them to get the slide right. That's not imprecision; that's honest fitting.

Two alignment warnings carry the build. Step 6: the center partition must be EXACTLY centered top to bottom and front to back, 'otherwise your drawers will slide cockeyed' — the partition is the drawer system's reference surface. And the drawers themselves are cut-to-fit with the cut list's own triple exclamation marks: measure between YOUR 2x2s, subtract 1/4", and build to that.

Note the leg detail in step 1: the end frames' outside and bottom edges are deliberately NOT flush — the aprons inset on the legs, and the bottom rails sit 3/4" up so the casters' load path runs through the legs. Buy a premade 24" x 52" project panel for the top and spend your patience on the drawers instead. This cut list carries the source's 2013 reader-feedback corrections — a plan that's been proofread by a decade of builders.

materials.name: Project panel, 3/4", 24" x 52"
materials.note: The top. Premade panel recommended — flat and ready.

materials.name: 2x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Top aprons, trim, and the drawer cleats.

materials.name: Casters
materials.note: Two locking minimum — a rolling island near a stove wants brakes.

materials.name: Candle wax
materials.note: The drawer-slide lubricant. Any plain candle.

cutList.note: NOT flush to the legs' outsides/bottoms — the inset is deliberate.
Both ends 45°, parallel, long point to long point.
The center one is the drawer reference — EXACTLY centered.
Fixed with pocket holes, or adjustable on shelf pins.
Two screws each at first — you WILL adjust them.
Drawers are CUT TO FIT — the list's numbers are guides only.

steps.title: Build the two X end frames
steps.body:
Build each end: legs, top and bottom rails — noting the deliberate insets; outside and bottom are NOT flush — with pocket holes and glue. Drill 3/4" pocket holes facing up on the insides of the top rails now (the source's tip) for attaching the top later.

Fit the X into each frame: full diagonal first, then the half pieces meeting it. Glue and screws.

steps.title: Join the ends
steps.body: Connect the leg sets with the bottom aprons — set 3/4" up from the actual leg bottoms — and the 2x2 top aprons, leaving the drawer gap between the front pair. The source added a third 2x2 at the back 'to square things up now'; do the same, and check diagonals while you still can.

steps.title: Shelf sides — the center EXACTLY centered
steps.body:
Install the three 1x12 verticals that form the drawer bays and shelf bay. The center partition must be exactly centered top-to-bottom and front-to-back — it's the reference every drawer measurement comes from, and the source's warning is blunt: off-center means cockeyed drawers forever.

Add the 1x10 backs behind the drawer bays.

steps.title: Slats and shelves
steps.body: Space the fourteen 1x3 slats evenly across the bottom shelf and nail them down. Install the two 1x12 shelves — pocket holes for fixed, shelf pins for adjustable; stockpots vote for adjustable.

steps.title: Cleats, wax, drawers
steps.body:
Mount the 2x2 drawer cleats with TWO screws each — the plan promises adjustment, so leave yourself the adjustment. Rub the cleat tops with candle wax.

Measure between the 2x2s, subtract 1/4", and build each drawer box to that width (pocket holes on the fronts/backs' outsides — the faces hide them), 1x2 cleats on the box sides riding the waxed 2x2s. Fit, adjust, wax again, repeat until they glide. Set the boxes 3/4" inset for the faces.

steps.title: Faces, top, casters
steps.body:
Cut the 1x8 drawer faces to their openings with 1/8" gaps and attach with finish nails and glue (screws from inside after). Frame the top's underside edge with the 1x2 trim, then land the project panel and fasten through the pocket holes you pre-drilled in step 1.

Bolt on the casters — at least two locking, because a rolling island in a kitchen full of people needs a parking brake. Wax anything that still whispers, stain everything, and roll it into service.

images.alt: Rolling rustic kitchen island with X-braced ends, drawers, and slatted shelf
